characters.name: Adarsh Upadhyaya
characters.title: The King of San Ramon
characters.role: Protagonist · 9th grader at Quarry Lane High School
characters.bio: Crowned by cafeteria consensus and group-chat decree, Adarsh rules San Ramon with quiet confidence, legendary homework stamina, and an uncanny ability to end hallway disputes with a single nod. His court meets near the window tables.

characters.name: Arjun Mehta
characters.title: The King of Dublin
characters.role: Rival sovereign · rumored Dublin High sophomore
characters.bio: Self-proclaimed ruler of Dublin and the eastern foothills. Commands the Dublin faction from a fortified study group at the public library. Known for aggressive snack-trade policy and sharp rhetoric on social media.

characters.name: Maya Chen
characters.title: Campus Correspondent
characters.bio: Covers cafeteria diplomacy, PE class alliances, and breaking hallway news. Has never missed a deadline except once during a fire drill.

characters.name: Jordan Reeves
characters.title: Kingdom Bureau Chief
characters.bio: Specializes in municipal events, festival coverage, and translating Patch headlines into royal implications for the San Ramon court.

characters.name: Priya Sharma
characters.title: War Desk Analyst
characters.bio: Tracks troop movements along the I-580 corridor, bus-stop skirmishes, and peace talks held in neutral Danville parking lots.

characters.name: Coach Martinez
characters.title: Minister of Physical Affairs
characters.bio: Witnessed the King's voluntary extra lap and declared it "aspirational." Handles ceasefire negotiations during dodgeball tournaments.

war.name: The Tri-Valley Succession Conflict
war.started: Fall 2025 — disputed claim to the 'Greater East Bay Crown'
war.summary: When Adarsh was crowned King of San Ramon, Arjun Mehta of Dublin rejected the coronation, citing historical Dublin claims to the entire Tri-Valley. What began as lunch-table trash talk escalated into a cold war of group-chat raids, strategic unfollows, and competing festival appearances.

fronts.name: The I-580 Corridor
fronts.description: Primary border between San Ramon and Dublin. Skirmishes occur at park-and-ride lots and boba shop neutral zones.

fronts.name: The Quarry Lane Line
fronts.description: Adarsh's home school sits near the border. Dublin scouts are frequently spotted at sporting events 'observing.'

fronts.name: The Festival Front
fronts.description: Both kings compete for visibility at San Ramon Art & Wind Festival, Dublin farmers markets, and Memorial Day ceremonies.

timeline.event: Adarsh crowned King of San Ramon after unanimous cafeteria vote (one abstention).
King of Dublin publishes rival manifesto in a Dublin group chat. San Ramon responds with memes.
The Great Boba Blockade — Dublin faction occupies a popular tea shop; peace restored after 47 minutes.
Neutral-zone summit in Danville fails when both kings arrive with incompatible homework schedules.
Art & Wind Festival détente: Adarsh and Arjun photographed twenty feet apart, not making eye contact.

war.current_status: Uneasy ceasefire. Both sides honor a mutual no-spoiler pact after 9 PM but remain one cafeteria incident from open conflict.
